# ProbateSwift — Harness Index

Generated rollup of `harness/` nodes. Strategy layer built by harness-discovery (2026-06-03);
delivery layer (solutions/decisions + opportunity `delivery:` verdicts) added by harness-builder
(2026-06-03) from the codebase. **2026-06-11:** David resolved the three open `needs_user_input`
items (pricing model, IHT form strategy, checkout) and added the browser-autofill solution idea.

## Graph tree (Problem → Opportunity → Solution → Decision)

- **Vision** — Affordable, personalised, self-serve probate for England & Wales `[vision]` ✅ approved
- **Goals** — coverage end-to-end · paying customers · lower time & cost `[goals]` ✅ approved
- **Market** — UK E&W probate & estate administration `[market]`

Problems → Opportunities → Solutions (●=in code ○=candidate/not built) → Decisions:

- `prob-dont-know-if-needed` — Don't know if probate is needed / eligible
- `prob-when-solicitor-needed` — No clear signal a case is too complex
  - → `opp-guided-triage` — Personalised triage **[delivery: built]**
    - ● `sol-rule-flag-engine` — Deterministic eligibility & flag engine
    - ● `sol-personalised-task-list` — Tasks generated from flags
    - ○ `sol-llm-triage` — LLM conversational triage (alternative)
    - ⚖ `dec-rule-engine-vs-llm-triage` — chose rule engine over LLM
    - ⚖ `dec-assessment-seeding` — landing assessment seeds in-app evaluation **(superseded)**
    - ⚖ `dec-customer-journey-intake` — **merged intake, hybrid triage, helper handoff (user-decided 2026-06-11)**

- `prob-stopped-applications` — Avoidable errors get applications stopped
- `prob-iht-forms-complexity` — IHT400 & schedules not fit for laypeople
  - → `opp-presubmission-validation` — Pre-submission validation **[delivery: partial]**
    - ● `sol-readiness-gate` — Blockers & missing-requirements gate
    - ● `sol-rule-flag-engine`, ● `sol-personalised-task-list`
  - → `opp-iht-form-assistant` — Guided IHT-form completion **[delivery: partial]**
    - ○ `sol-guided-iht400` — Full guided completion w/ prefill → outputs completed PDFs **(CHOSEN, not built)**
    - ● `sol-iht-flagging-bundled-forms` — IHT routing + bundled GOV.UK PDFs (current, superseded as v1 target)
    - ○ `sol-pa1p-browser-autofill` — Chrome extension autofills GOV.UK online probate (fast PA1P route)
    - ⚖ `dec-iht-form-strategy` — **resolved**: fill-for-you (PDFs, not e-filing); IHT400 paper-only, PA1P via PDF or extension

- `prob-emotional-admin-burden` — Death admin exhausting & fragmented
  - → `opp-estate-dashboard` — Track assets/people/docs/tasks **[delivery: built]**
    - ● `sol-estate-dashboard` — Cases/people/assets/liabilities/docs/tasks
    - ● `sol-doc-ai-extraction` — Gemini auto-populate from uploads
    - ● `sol-milestone-gamification` — Game-like milestone progression
    - ⚖ `dec-doc-ai-gemini` — chose Gemini 2.0 Flash for extraction

- `prob-cost-of-professionals` — Professional probate expensive / opaque
  - → `opp-flat-fee-positioning` — Low flat fee vs % pricing **[delivery: not_started]**
    - ○ `sol-flat-fee-checkout` — single SKU, gated at submission; checkout not built
    - ⚖ `dec-pricing-model` — **resolved**: £295 flat (committed, acquisition-first) · charge at submission · one paid SKU (no free tier)

Infrastructure decisions (no direct opportunity): `dec-auth-clerk` (Clerk over Stytch/Replit),
`dec-hosting-railway` (Railway over Replit).

Competitors (context, pricing refreshed 2026-06-11): `comp-govuk-diy` (~£316 DIY),
`comp-budget-grant-services` (£195–£355 floor), `comp-octopus-legacy` (grant £585 no-IHT / £1,290 with
IHT forms / full admin £2,730+ — primary threat), `comp-farewill` (Essential ~£595–£895; Complete £1,500), `comp-kings-court-trust`
(full admin £2,340+, avg £4,190–£7,321), `comp-coop-legal` (~2.5–4% of estate),
`comp-banks-percentage` (2–4% of estate).

## Resolved 2026-06-11 (David) — now `ready_for_review`, promote to `approved` when happy
- **`dec-pricing-model`** — **£295 flat, committed** (acquisition-first; price test deferred until there's a user
  base). Charge **at submission** (free to use until then). **One paid SKU, no separate free tier.**
- **`dec-iht-form-strategy`** — Chose **fill-for-you** (`sol-guided-iht400`). "Fill" = **output completed PDFs**, not
  e-filing: **IHT400 is paper-only** (print, wet-sign, post); **PA1P** online service has no API, so either prefill the
  paper PA1P PDF or use a **Chrome extension** to autofill the GOV.UK service (`sol-pa1p-browser-autofill`, David's idea).
  Optional print-and-post fulfilment as an upsell.
- **`sol-flat-fee-checkout`** — Model settled (single SKU, gated at submission); checkout still to build, price number TBD.

## Open follow-ups (build/validation, not blocking)
- Sequence the PA1P route: paper-PDF first vs browser-extension as a fast-route upgrade.
- Decide print-and-post: v1 or later upsell. Legal/liability review of generating signable form content.

## `assumption`-level nodes (not yet grounded in code — validate before relying on)
- `sol-llm-triage` (alternative not built), `sol-flat-fee-checkout` (price number untested),
  `sol-pa1p-browser-autofill` (idea, not built), `dec-pricing-model` (price number untested).
